inline_comments.message: there's no optionality, it's done whatever is config.

Indeed, it could be skipped completely, but because both defaults are defaults - (/etc/kubernetes is created regardless of this, and timeout of 2m is set also regardless of this) - and the rest of config variables is set to defaults (for obvious reasons) - I see no such problem with it - but I could make it optional and add yet again some new fancy variable or overload one of these like: if they are empty do nothing.

Ok it would make a hell a lot more sense if documenting what you just said. I.e. these steps does not change default functionality at all. Unless user adds something into template file.

With that documented it's ok.

Here what you highlighted does not actually change behaviour but this change is not limited to this file - in cluster.yml.j2 it supports now dynamic/runtime configuration -for that is that other extra arg... and how to leverage it is linked in the commit message.
